description: A13FJ - Day 6, part 3: Worn Out Crew

tokens: The crew of Apollo 13 has just spent a number of hours receiving and writing down the reactivation checklist that is to be used to bring the Command Module Odyssey back to life after several days of cold, powerless hibernation.
They have recharged the reentry batteries and have made plans for stowing their equipment for the landing.
Everything is still looking good, and the crew is holding on, but the extreme stress, lack of rest, proper meals and adequate water is starting to show its toll.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: First thing is we need a docking angle check as soon as you can conveniently get it.
Second point, you're free to drink all the descent stage water you want, if that wasn't clear before.
And the third point, if you have a piece of paper, stand by to get writer's cramps.
We have something to give you here.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: You want the - again a look at the docking tunnel index?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Fred sounds puzzled by the request.
The docking index was reported to Mission Control early on in the mission, and is not expected to change.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Why don't you go ahead and look at that...
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 128 hours.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Jim says the docking tunnel index is a plus 2.1 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: He says make that sign a minus.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We just changed, plus to minus.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: And your second item was, we can drink all that descent water that's left - look like about 5 percent, and I'll get book here and be ready to start writing.
transcript.speaker: Swigert

tokens: Get - This won't be as long as - as the one T.
read up, but you'll need several pages, probably.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: I've got lots of pages here of Ken's photo log set to go.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This is "Entry, LM Prep." Starts EI minus 6 hours.
At that time, you power up the AGS.
I think I'll give you a line at a time and, when I hear you say okay on that, I'll go to the next.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: This checklist will be the Lunar Module side of the reentry preparations, performed simultaneously with the Command Module procedures.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Hey, how do you read now?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Battery 5 Normal Feed, On is the first step.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Use modified LM DPS/RCS 30-minute activation checklist.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: And, Fred, the time on LM preentry procedure is just ...
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: The time on the LM preentry procedure is just as soon as you finish midcourse-7, or 5 hours ...
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: I've got this TEI minus 5 hours, MCC-7 if required, and now you're going to start me a new procedure called LM preentry procedure.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: The maneuver will be performed by the LM.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Under that, Dead Band, Max; and Attitude Control, three, to Pulse.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: The LM's attitude control will be set to maximum deadband - which allows for more rotation away from the desired attitude - and to use pulse mode, for very short firings of the RCS jets.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Maneuver back to SM Sep attitude.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Should the brief firing of the Command Module thrusters cause them to veer away from their desired attitude, the LM will then maneuver back.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: And under that, Attitude Control, Pitch and Roll, Pulse; Yaw, Mode Control.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Mode Control sets for automatic attitude control under either the computer or the AGS.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: That's EI minus 4½ hours, configure for SM Sep.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: That's affirm, and under that, Balance Couple, On.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: This switch selects whether paired or single RCS jets are used for controlling pitch and roll.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: AGS 404, plus 0; 405, plus 0; 406, plus 0; and 470, read out.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: In this step, they input four commands into the DEDA to program the AGS.
The first three settings will set up the AGS to sense their acceleration on all three axes, and then the selection of address 470 will display the acceleration on the X axis, ie.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Translate plus-X at 0.5 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: This is to change their velocity by half a foot.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Once the Service Module has been separated, Fred will use the RCS jets to return to their previous velocity.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Attitude Control, three of them, to Pulse.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Pitch to acquire Service Module for photos.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: We can use the ACA now for control.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Next step will be moving the Lunar Module to try and photograph the damage to the Service Module.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: EI minus 3 hours, maneuver and Att Hold for Moon view, attitude...
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Was that maneuver in Att Hold for Moon view.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: - For Moon view attitude until CM platform release.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: It says maneuver in Att Hold for Moon view attitude until CM platform release.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Better worded, it would have been maneuver and hold in attitude for Moon view, et cetera.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: That's mainly for Jack to be able to get it in the optics, you mean.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Could you give us a readback on everything up to this point?
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: I'll go back to Normal Voice.
It, might - Or is this pretty clear?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: The master title, "Entry, LM Prep, EI Minus 6 hours." First, Bat 5 Normal Feed, On; second, use modified LM DPS/RCS, 30-minute activation checklist.
Next, is at TEI minus 5 hours, MCC-7, if required.
Now, we have a LM preentry procedure.
First, we maneuver to Service Module Sep attitude.
Under that, Dead Band Max, and Attitude Control, three, to Pulse.
Fourth, we - The LM maneuvers back to SM Sep attitude, with Attitude Controls, Pitch and Roll to Pulse, Attitude Control, Yaw, to Mode Control.
Now, you got that so far, Vance.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: At EI minus 4½ hours, configure for SM Sep.
Secondly, zero the counters in AGS; 404, 405, 406, plus 0; read out 470.
Thirdly, translate plus-X to 0.5 foot per second.
Sixth, Attitude Control, three, to Pulse.
Seventh, pitch to acquire Service Module for photos.
And a note, use the ACA for control.
Last, we had - you gave me was TEI minus 3 hours.
Maneuver in Att Hold for Moon view until the CM platform release.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Only - Only a minor point that we just picked up.
At the very top, EI minus 6 hours, the title is "power up," above Bat 5 Normal Feed, On.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: The title above Bat 5 Normal Feed, On, is "power up."
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: And on the last, it was maneuver and Att Hold for Moon view attitude until CM platform release.
I think that's what you read.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Next is TEI minus what, Vance?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: It's - It's EI minus 2½ hours, Fred.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: The title, "Power removal from CM LM umbilical." [Long pause.]
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Next, assumes - This assumes all descent batteries on line.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: I'll put that as a note.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: And Ascent Batteries 5 and 6 on Normal Feed.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: This assumes all descent Bats on line, and Ascent Bats 5 and 6 on Normal Feed.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: All six LM batteries should be online at this point.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Next, CSM CB LM Power 1, Main B to Open.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: CSM, CB LM Power 1, Main B, Open.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: CB, LM Power 2, Main B, Open.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: CSM, CB LM Power 2, Main B, Open.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: CB EPS Sensor Signal, Main B to Open.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: CB EPS Sensor Signal, Main B, Open.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: These steps in the Command Module remove power from the umbilicals connecting the two spacecraft.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: CB, EPS panels 11 and 16, Cross Tie Balance Loads to Close.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: CB EPS 11, panels 11 and 16, Cross Tie Bal Loads, Close.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: These breakers allow the two main buses in the LM to be connected together.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Next, CB EPS panel 16, Bat Feed Tie, two of them, Open.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: LM, CB EPS, panel 16, Bat Feed Tie, two, Open.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This opens the crosstie battery feeds.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: CB EPS 16, Ascent ECA Control to Close.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: CB EPS panel 16, Ascent ECA Control, Close.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Battery 1, Hi Volt - High Voltage to Off/Reset.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Battery 5, Hi Volt - or, it should be Battery 5 Normal Feed to Off/Reset.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I think that's what he meant.
transcript.speaker: Lovell

tokens: Battery 5, Normal Feed - Normal Feed, Off/Reset.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Bat 2, Low Voltage, Off/Reset then On.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Battery 2, Low Volt, Off/Reset, then On.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Bat 2, Hi Volt, Off/Reset, then On.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: I didn't catch your readback, Fred.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: The last item was item 9, and that was Bat 2, Low Volt, Off/Reset, then On.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: After that, I gave another one.
Bat 2, Hi Volt, Off/Reset, then On.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Bat 2, Hi Volt, Off/Reset, then On.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: CB, EPS, 16, Bat Feed Tie, two of them, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: CB, EPS, 16, Bat Feed Tie, two, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This long litany resets and then reconnects the batteries to the buses.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The LMP should transfer to the CM.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: And just before LMP transfer to CM, we'd like to have you add another step, Fred.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: That is, CB, EPS, Ascent ECA Control, Open.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: You want CB - CB, EPS, that's on 16, I assume, Ascent ECA Control, Open.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This is EI minus 1 hour 45 minutes, next.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Maneuver to CM LM Jett attitude.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: And you'll have a PAD for that attitude.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Maneuver to CM LM Jett attitude.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Matter of fact, Jack has a listing of your PADs there, Freddo, so you can review them after this is over.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: This refers to Command Module's attitude control selection switch.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This is for the Command Module?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: The step above for CM free was Command Module, but the Comm is for LM again.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: S-Band, PM, Primary, Primary again, Off, PCM, Range, Off, and Hi.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Our S-Band Config is: PM, Prim, Prim, Off, PCM, Range, Off, Hi.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This comm configuration selects PM mode for the radio, Primary transceiver, primary power amplifier, Voice Off, telemetry On, ranging Off, and high bit rate.
Vance listed the choices in the order the comm selection switches are arranged in the console.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Do you have any druthers about which Omni?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Select - Did you say Aft, or best?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I was wondering if that's still going to be best after it leaves - But, okay.
Select best Omni at the time.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Next comes the configuration for the Environmental Control System.
It will have little to do in terms of life support once the crew is gone, but will maintain operational temperature of the equipment until the end.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Under ECS, Forward Dump valve, Close.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Close setting on the dump valve prevents onboard atmosphere from being vented out even if the pressure rises beyond the normal limit.
During typical operations, this valve would be used to depressurize the cabin while on the lunar surface.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: In Auto mode, the suit circuit will be relieved into the cabin if the pressure rises too high.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This closes the valve that allows cabin air to flow into the suit circuit for replenishment.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This prevents the suit circuit air from flowing into the cabin.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Suit Isolation valves, two of them, Disconnect.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: ISO valves, two, to Suit Disconnect.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: These close the oxygen flow into the suit hoses.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Pressure Regs A and B, Close.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Regs A and B to Close.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This stops all oxygen flow into the suit circuit.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Closing the repress valve prevents oxygen flow into the cabin.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Finally, they turn off the suit fan.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Maybe you need a scroll, yet.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: I want to ask him why all these steps just to get rid of a LM that's going to burn up in a half hour.
transcript.speaker: Lovell

tokens: Although Jim was ostensibly resting, he has been roused by the continous radio chatter and the Vox Fred turned on earlier picks up his outburst, heard muffled and not meant for Mission Control to hear.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: The last one was Suit Fan, Off.
Hey, Jim, Deke said he thought you were asleep.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Vance tries to be as diplomatic as possible, and implies that the astronaut boss Deke Slayton has already given his word on the matter.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: All these steps woke me up.
transcript.speaker: Lovell

tokens: I got my audio breaker on.
transcript.speaker: Lovell

tokens: Did you get Suit Fan, Off?
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Circuit breakers, panel - circuit breaker panel 16 rather.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: CB panel 16, ECS, Cabin Repress, Open.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This pulls power from the automatic cabin repress system.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Maybe Jim has a point there, though.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Overhead Dump Valve, Close, and it is already.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This valve is in the docking hatch into the tunnel that leads to the Command Module.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Next, Attitude Control, three, to Mode Control.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: We'd like to - change the position of that Attitude Control, three, Mode Control to be just under CM free.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Under, it'll be my item B, after verify CM free, it's Att Control, three, to Mode Control.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: And, along with that, Dead Band, Max.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Three to Mode Control, comma, Dead Band to Max.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: If I don't sound too clear, it's because I'm holding a flashlight between my teeth.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I think I'm - I'm up with you.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Now again down at the bottom beneath ECS, verify transfer list.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: IVT to CM, and close hatch.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: What was that - What was the first part of that?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Or rather, IVT, that's the Commander to the CM, and close hatch.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Actually, it's commander transfer to CM and you mean close hatches.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: That's all for the time line, Fred.
Now the contingency checklist, if it's handy.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Now, at this point, you have this section marked up already, so what I give you now represents additional Deltas to your previously marked up checklist.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: I've got a different color pen, so it'll show up nicely.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Do you mean, under IVT to the LM, number 4, RCS System A/B-2, Quads 4, Auto?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: It's the - the only 4 on that page 24 and it's a - presently has after it RCS System A/B-2, Quads, 4, to Auto.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: ...put verify AGS Status switch to Off.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: At the bottom of the page - Okay.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: First row is okay as is.
Second row, under Flight Displays, GASTA should be closed.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Under AC Bus A, close GASTA.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: GASTA Flight Displays and AC Bus A will be closed.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: That's all on the second line.
Now the third line, under Stability and Control, ATCA (PGNS) to closed.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Fourth line, near the right side, LGC Standby to Close.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Fourth row is okay as is.
Now we would like to add - add in a late arrival to row two.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Under Flight Displays, Mission Timer, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: We can look at the ghosties right to the end.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: That's all for that page, Fred.
That should add up to six circuit breakers all closed, on that page.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: They add a few circuit breakers with the intention of providing more telemetry data from the LM after they separate.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Have we looked seriously now at just the essential things required to make a safe LM jettison?
We're not - I don't think we have the time to really do any engineering data that people might want to look at as this thing goes into the atmosphere.
transcript.speaker: Lovell

tokens: After his earlier outburst, Lovell has now come on the line to speak his mind directly to Mission Control.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: I - I think that that was what everybody aimed for, and people down here feel that it's the essentials, that it's what you need.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Well, we'll do everything you think essential, but I just don't want to be throwing switches at the last minute.
What we're really thinking about, getting that Command Module in good shape.
transcript.speaker: Lovell

tokens: The only reason we're bringing up the PGNS is to have another reference system just to control the LM when you jettison the thing.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: It's - I thought the - an AGS Att Hold would have been sufficient for this, when we first thought about this.
transcript.speaker: Lovell

tokens: The arrival of the Astronaut Office boss Tom Stafford has made Jim sound more diplomatic than before.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: But we decided just in case we have any glitch with the AGS, we want to make sure we get that LM off in a good shape.
And I agree with you on keeping out all the garbage on it.
transcript.speaker: Stafford

tokens: Hey, would you like to have...
transcript.speaker: Slayton

tokens: Would you like to have us play you some guitar music to put you to sleep?
transcript.speaker: Slayton

tokens: (Laughter) Oh, you know how to get to a guy.
transcript.speaker: Lovell

tokens: We'll even have a 12-string guitar for you, Jimbo.
transcript.speaker: Slayton

tokens: Deke Slayton returns to the airwaves to deliver a joke that the editing team has not been able to decipher.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Lovell's outburst was dramatized for the Apollo 13 movie.
The movie adaptation could even be called somewhat toned down, surprisingly enough, consisting of a brief monologue from Lovell about demanding the procedures to be ready sooner rather than later.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: At the top of the page, under step 4, just above "VHF/S-band activation," put in, "Delete Power AMP and LGC." These are caution lights.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Under "VHF/S-band activation and checkout," step 2, we have changes to the Comm as follows.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Scratch - Scratch out the Down and Down Voice.
That leaves - That'll give you Voice.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: That's the way we had it last time.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: And, we'd like to insert Ranging.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Under "PGNS turn-on," add in steps 1 and 2 again, which are No Attitude light off, Verb 96 Enter, and set Event Timer.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: And underneath "Set Event Timer," add the following: Verb 25 Noun 07, Enter; 1257, Enter.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: If you got the 1257, Enter, then 252, Enter and Enter.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: After event, set Event Timer, it's Verb 25 Noun 07, Enter; 1257, Enter; 252 Enter; Enter.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: And this activates your upfiring jets.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: What this does, Fred, is activate upfiring jets.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: They're all - actually, all vertical jets.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Next, the same page, under "DAP set gimbal drive," go to step 2.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Proceeding on down below the Noun 47, to the Proceed, insert Verb 34, Enter, after the Proceed.
And that would come before the Noun 48.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: To Pro and then do a Verb 34.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We have some noise here, Fred.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: At the very first step above "Guidance Control, AGS," put in Verb 76, Enter.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Below "Guidance Control, AGS," the third step should be changed to be Mode Control, both, to Att Hold.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: You want Mode Control, both, to Att Hold.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: At minus 6 - 6 hours - or 6 minutes rather, under Mode Control, PGNS, Att Hold, which was scratched out, insert Verb 41 Noun 20, Enter, and three more Enters.
So - So it's as shown in your procedure there.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Below that, put in DEDA, 400 plus 5.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: We want back in again the next step which is "At burn attitude: Verb 40 Noun 20, Enter." [Pause.]
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Proceeding on down, delete "At burn attitude" and the step above it, which is 400 plus 5.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Under 4 minutes, minus 4 minutes, add back in Mode Control, both, to Att Hold.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Mode Control, both, to Att Hold.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Two steps below that, add back in TTCA Commander Throttle min DPS, and add in DPS.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Seems like you should be in Jets, since this is going to be - is this going to be RCS or a DPS burn?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This will be Jets for RCS and Throttle for DPS.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: What I don't understand is, what is this SPS likely to be?
I thought it was going to be an RCS.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Well, we - We expect that, but we're remaining flexible.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: I'll put in TTCA Throttle min for DPS, Jets for RCS.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Beneath everything on that page, add in the following.
Return to "Entry, - LM Prep" check - return to "Entry, LM Prep" checklist...
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Hold on, Vance; I don't understand.
We want to delete everything on page 34?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: What we want to do is leave everything the way it is on 34, and add this one comment I'm giving you at the - at the bottom of it.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Go ahead with your added comment.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: [Garble] turn to - return to "Entry, LM checklist?"
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Let me take a look and see how that flows, now.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: That would be the LM - the LM preentry procedure.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: That's correct, and, also on that page, you - from our last correct - or addition exercise, you have a section called "Reestablish PTC." We'd like to scratch all of that, of course.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: That's all except for one comment that we have, and - Just a second.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Fred, we're polling the room for comments here, any last minute thoughts from people.
We'll be right back with you.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Just two changes, comments to what we just gave you.
The first is near the front at EI - in the section under "EI minus 2½ hours, power removal from CM LM umbilical." We'll let you turn to that.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Okay, I'm there at "EI minus 2½ hours, power removal CM LM umbilical."
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Under LM, we have a step, Bat 2, Low Voltage, Off/Reset, than On.
Next we have a step Bat 2, Hi Voltage.
We request that you wait in between these two steps for us to give you a Go.
In other words, we'd like to look and see that all the relays closed and all that sort of thing before you throw Bat 2, Hi Voltage, to Off/Reset, and On.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Okay, After Bat 2, Low Voltage, Off/Reset, I'll - then On, I'll wait for a MSFN Go before proceeding.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Next, at - Under the section of "EI minus 1 hour and 45 minutes" - No, change that.
Correction, "EI minus 1 hour, 35 minutes," refer to the ECS section, and we have a change.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: We should cross out all this stuff with a couple of exceptions.
Forward Dump valve, Closed, the first step, should remain.
All other steps should be scratched, except for the last one, which is Overhead Dump valve, Closed.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Under ECS, we delete all except the first step, Forward Dump valve, Close, and step 10, which is Overhead Dump valve, Closed, and leave in verify transfer list.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Leave in what follows, which is verify transfer list and IVT and all that.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: IVT, of course, meaning CDR transferring to the CM.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: IVT stands for Intravehicular Transfer and is NASA lingo for 'moving from one spaceship to another'.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Not quite as extensive as the CSM had, but still pretty long.
Any comments or questions to the house here?
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: No, it looks pretty good to me, Vance.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Only other comment, aside from the checklist, we've been watching your descent stage water, and it looks - or your ascent stage water, and it looks real good.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: We're going to try to refrain from calling you from now on, so that you can maybe get a couple of winks.
We're getting a status around the room first, to see if anybody has any comments for you and, after that, we'll try to leave you alone for awhile.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: We don't have much for you.
Only comments as follows: your consumables are looking good; your entry gamma right now is minus 6.01.
We're expecting a midcourse of 2.81 foot per second which should be about 21 seconds of RCS.
transcript.speaker: Brand

tokens: Request you open the Power Amplifier circuit breaker and Down Voice Backup, please.
transcript.speaker: Lousma

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 129 hours, 43 minutes.
Recounting events since the change of shift about 3 hours ago.
We've completed passing up to the crew checklists for both the Command and Service Module and the Lunar Module - entry checklist.
At about 128 hours, 30 minutes we had the crew switch over from the descent water tanks which were down to about 5 per cent to the LM ascent stage water tank for cooling.
We instructed the crew that they could drink all the water from the descent tank.
At about 127 hours, 59 minutes, the battery charging was terminated.
This had been accomplished by using power from the LM batteries and using that to charge the Command Module entry batteries, two of the three CM entry batteries.
At the present time, Fred Haise is on watch, and Jim Lovell and Jack Swigert are scheduled for about 5 hours rest.
We're also planning to place a minimum of calls to the spacecraft to allow Fred Haise to get a bit of rest also, at this time.
Flight Dynamics Officer, and the Retro Officer - the Return to Earth Officer have currently predicted that our Entry Interface angle will be minus 6.03 degrees without a mid-course correction.
We're presently planning for a mid-course correction - Mid-course correction 7 which would be 5 hours prior to Entry Interface.
And as it looks right now, this mid-course correction would be quite small to put us on the desired flight path angle of about 6½ degrees - negative 6½ degrees interface angle.
To accomplish this, it looks as if we'll have about a 2.8 second - 2.8 foot per second burn with the Lunar Module Reaction Control System thrusters.
This would require about about 21 seconds of thrusting.
During the course of the evening, both Deke Slayton and Tom Stafford have been standing by the CapCom console.

At one point, while Fred Haise was receiving a Lunar Module entry checklist, with a hot mike, with his mike open, we heard a comment from Lovell which prompted a remark from Deke Slayton to the effect that he would play up some guitar music if they thought that would soothe Lovell into getting a bit of rest.
Ken Mattingly has also been here a good part of the evening.
We don't see him at the console at this time.
Our Capsule Communicator up until just a few minutes ago was Astronaut Vance Brand and he has since turned over those duties to Astronaut Jack Lousma.
At 129 hours, 46 minutes; Apollo 13 is 87,684 nautical miles - correction 74,900 nautical miles from the Earth, and travelling at a speed of 6,999 feet per second.
All of the consumables aboard the LM remain in good shape at this time.

We had Fred Haise remove the LM power amplifier and in effect reduce the effective power output - radio frequency power output from the LM from about 20 watts to about 1 watt, which is the normal powered down configuration we've been in with the LM for communications.
We expect it will, as it has in the past, give us periods of noisy communications as the LM antennas rotate through the so-called null areas, where the signal to noise ratio drops off.
We get a high noise level and this will persist until the spacecraft rotates through to where we have good lockup on the LM antenna.
And again at 129 hours, 47 minutes; this is Apollo Control, Houston.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Distance is now 138,715 km, speed, 2,133 m/s.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 130 hours, 36 minutes.
The Flight Dynamics Officer just reported that continued tracking data shows the spacecraft to be within the entry corridor still at about the same Entry Interface angle as previously reported.
The tracking at this time shows that our Entry Interface angle remains at about minus 6.03 degrees.
The midcourse correction plan for 5 hours prior to reentry which will be about 7 hours, 3 minutes from now.
It is planned at this time to be 2.81 feet per second.
That would be about a 21 second burn with the spacecraft, the LM Reaction Control System thrusters.

In about an hour, Flight Director Milton Windler plans to go over once again with the flight controllers all of the step by step procedures which the crew will be following tomorrow prior to entry while with the Command and Service Module.
At the present time, Apollo 13 is 71,483 nautical miles from the Earth; traveling at a speed of 7,192 feet per second.
All of our consumables on the LM remain unchanged.
We're in good shape on water, battery power, and lithium hydroxide.
The present level of carbon dioxide as measured in the spacecraft telemetered back to the ground is about 4 tenth of a millimeter of mercury which is very good.
We're showing a total average current on the LM, at this time, of between 10 and 12 amps, which has been the normal amount we've been running in the powered down mode with the Lunar Module.
At the present time, all 3 of the astronauts are apparently resting.
Astronauts Lovell and Swigert were to have begun a rest period at about 128 hours, 17 minutes.
We did hear once or twice Jim Lovell after that period.

At the 129 hours, 39 minutes or about 1 hours ago, we completed passing the Lunar Module a preentry checklist up to Fred Haise and advised him that we would keep communications to a minimum, and hoped he would be able to get a little rest also.
At 130 hours, 39 minutes; this is Mission Control, Houston.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 131 hours, 14 minutes.
Our last conversation with the spacecraft came at about 129 hours, 39 minutes; a little more than an hour and a half ago.
Since that time it's been relatively quiet here in Mission Control.
Within the next hour, Flight Director Milton Windler plans for review again the procedures to be followed during the reentry with each of the flight controllers.
These entry procedures were passed up to the crew, prior to the time they began their rest period.
At this time we presume that all three crewmen aboard the spacecraft are resting.
At the present time we show Apollo 13 to be 68,857 nautical miles from the Earth.
The velocity increase now up to 7,346 feet per second and increasing ever more rapidly as the spacecraft nears Earth.
We're now 6 hours, 25 minutes from the scheduled midcourse correction which will put Apollo 13 in the center of the entry corridor.
And we're 11 hours, 25 minutes from the beginning of reentry.
At 131 hours, 16 minutes; this is Mission Control, Houston.
transcript.speaker: PAO
